headline: Malaysia Reverses UN Vote on Nuclear Weapons Ban
context: Malaysia voted against UN General Assembly resolution on nuclear weapons ban enforcement in October 2025 reversing traditional position.

cards.big: Malaysia reversed position voting against UN nuclear weapons ban
cards.sub: Vote surprised diplomats as Malaysia championed disarmament since 1996

cards.big: Malaysia supported nuclear-free Southeast Asia since SEANWFZ in 1995
cards.sub: Vote reversal came after intense US and France lobbying

cards.big: Wisma Putra cited changed geopolitical landscape for the shift
cards.sub: Critics say Malaysia sacrificed principled stance for defence benefits

cards.big: 82 nations voted in favour while 35 including Malaysia voted against
cards.sub: NAM members expressed disappointment at Malaysia departure from consensus

cards.big: Nuclear vote tests whether principled foreign policy survives strategic relationship pressures
Reversing the nuclear weapons ban vote signals shifting alignment priorities over disarmament principles.
